questions.question: A 5 kg box is pushed across a floor with a net force of 20 N. What is the acceleration of the box?
questions.explanation: Using Newton's Second Law: a = F/m = 20 N / 5 kg = 4 m/s squared.

questions.question: A car traveling at 30 m/s comes to a complete stop in 6 seconds. What is the magnitude of its deceleration?
questions.explanation: Deceleration = change in velocity / time = 30 m/s / 6 s = 5 m/s squared.

questions.question: According to Newton's Third Law, when a hammer strikes a nail, the nail exerts a force on the hammer that is:
choices: Equal in magnitude and opposite in direction
Equal in magnitude and same direction
questions.explanation: Newton's Third Law states that for every action there is an equal and opposite reaction. The nail pushes back on the hammer with equal magnitude but opposite direction.

questions.question: A 10 kg object is at rest on a table. The coefficient of static friction between the object and the table is 0.4. What minimum horizontal force is needed to start the object moving? (g = 10 m/s squared)
questions.explanation: Friction force = coefficient x normal force = 0.4 x (10 kg x 10 m/s squared) = 0.4 x 100 N = 40 N.

questions.question: A ball is thrown straight up with an initial velocity of 20 m/s. Ignoring air resistance, approximately how high does it rise? (g = 10 m/s squared)
questions.explanation: At the highest point, velocity = 0. Using v squared = v0 squared - 2gh: 0 = 400 - 2(10)h, so h = 400/20 = 20 m.

questions.question: A worker lifts a 25 kg crate 2 meters off the ground. How much work does the worker do against gravity? (g = 10 m/s squared)
questions.explanation: Work = force x distance = (25 kg x 10 m/s squared) x 2 m = 250 N x 2 m = 500 J.

questions.question: A machine does 3000 J of work in 15 seconds. What is its power output?
questions.explanation: Power = work / time = 3000 J / 15 s = 200 W.

questions.question: A 0.5 kg ball moving at 8 m/s strikes a wall and bounces back at 6 m/s. What is the magnitude of the change in momentum?
questions.explanation: Change in momentum = m(v_final - v_initial). Taking the initial direction as positive: 0.5(-6 - 8) = 0.5(-14) = -7 kg m/s. The magnitude is 7 kg m/s.

questions.question: An object is in free fall near Earth's surface. After 3 seconds, approximately how fast is it moving? (g = 10 m/s squared, starting from rest)
questions.explanation: v = g x t = 10 m/s squared x 3 s = 30 m/s.

questions.question: A 2000 kg car is traveling at 10 m/s. What is its kinetic energy?
A spring with a spring constant of 400 N/m is compressed 0.1 m. How much potential energy is stored in the spring?
questions.explanation: PE = (1/2)k x squared = 0.5 x 400 x 0.01 = 2 J.

questions.question: Two ice skaters push off from each other. Skater A (60 kg) moves backward at 2 m/s. If Skater B has a mass of 40 kg, how fast does Skater B move?
questions.explanation: By conservation of momentum: m_A x v_A = m_B x v_B, so 60 x 2 = 40 x v_B, giving v_B = 120/40 = 3 m/s.

questions.question: A projectile is launched horizontally from a cliff. Which statement is true about its motion?
choices: Horizontal velocity increases due to gravity
Vertical velocity remains constant during flight
Horizontal velocity remains constant if air resistance is ignored
The projectile follows a straight-line path
questions.explanation: With no air resistance, there is no horizontal force, so horizontal velocity remains constant throughout the flight. Gravity only affects vertical motion.

questions.question: A crane lifts a 500 kg beam at a constant velocity. The tension in the cable must be:
choices: Greater than the weight of the beam
Less than the weight of the beam
Equal to the weight of the beam
Zero because the velocity is constant
questions.explanation: At constant velocity, acceleration is zero, so the net force is zero. The tension must equal the weight of the beam (Newton's First Law).

questions.question: A force of 50 N is applied to a 10 kg object on a frictionless surface. The object also has a 20 N force applied in the opposite direction. What is the acceleration?
questions.explanation: Net force = 50 N - 20 N = 30 N. Acceleration = 30 N / 10 kg = 3 m/s squared.

questions.question: A first-class lever has its fulcrum placed 0.5 m from a 200 N load and 2 m from the effort. What effort force is needed to balance the load?
questions.explanation: For a lever: effort x effort arm = load x load arm. Effort x 2 = 200 x 0.5, so effort = 100/2 = 50 N.

questions.question: A single fixed pulley is used to lift a 100 N weight. Ignoring friction, what effort force is required?
questions.explanation: A single fixed pulley changes direction of force only; it provides no mechanical advantage. The effort equals the load: 100 N.

questions.question: A system of pulleys has a mechanical advantage of 4. To lift a 600 N load, how much effort force is needed? (Ignore friction)
questions.explanation: Effort = Load / MA = 600 N / 4 = 150 N.

questions.question: An inclined plane is 6 m long and 2 m high. What is its ideal mechanical advantage?
questions.explanation: IMA of an inclined plane = length / height = 6 m / 2 m = 3.

questions.question: A wheelbarrow is an example of which class of lever?
questions.explanation: A wheelbarrow has the fulcrum at the wheel, the load in the center, and the effort at the handles -- this is a second-class lever where the load is between the fulcrum and effort.

questions.question: A screw has 8 threads per centimeter. If the screwdriver handle has a radius of 1.5 cm, what is the approximate ideal mechanical advantage? (Use MA = 2 x pi x r x threads per cm)
questions.explanation: MA = 2 x pi x r x threads per cm = 2 x 3.14159 x 1.5 x 8 = approximately 75.4.

questions.question: A wedge is 20 cm long and 4 cm thick at the base. What is its ideal mechanical advantage?
questions.explanation: IMA of a wedge = length / thickness = 20 cm / 4 cm = 5.

questions.question: A worker uses a 2 m long lever to lift a rock. The fulcrum is 0.4 m from the rock. What is the mechanical advantage?
questions.explanation: The effort arm is 2 m - 0.4 m = 1.6 m. MA = effort arm / load arm = 1.6 / 0.4 = 4.

questions.question: In a compound pulley system with 6 supporting ropes, what is the mechanical advantage?
questions.explanation: In a pulley system, the mechanical advantage equals the number of supporting ropes. With 6 supporting ropes, MA = 6.

questions.question: A machine has an actual mechanical advantage of 3 and an ideal mechanical advantage of 4. What is its efficiency?
questions.explanation: Efficiency = (AMA / IMA) x 100% = (3/4) x 100% = 75%.

questions.question: A driving gear with 15 teeth meshes with a driven gear with 45 teeth. If the driving gear rotates at 600 RPM, what is the speed of the driven gear?
questions.explanation: Gear ratio = driven teeth / driving teeth = 45/15 = 3. Output speed = input speed / gear ratio = 600/3 = 200 RPM.

questions.question: In the previous question, how does the torque on the driven gear compare to the torque on the driving gear?
questions.explanation: When speed decreases by a factor of 3, torque increases by the same factor (assuming no losses). The driven gear has three times the torque.

questions.question: Two meshing spur gears rotate in:
choices: The same direction only if gear sizes are equal
questions.explanation: Two meshing spur gears always rotate in opposite directions. The teeth interlock and push each other in opposing rotational directions.

questions.question: A gear train has three gears: Gear A (20 teeth) drives Gear B (40 teeth), which drives Gear C (10 teeth). If Gear A turns clockwise at 100 RPM, in which direction and at what speed does Gear C turn?
questions.explanation: A to B reverses direction (counterclockwise), B to C reverses again (clockwise). For speed: the overall ratio from A to C considers only the first and last gear in a simple gear train. Ratio = teeth_A / teeth_C = 20/10 = 2, so C speed = 100 x 2 = 200 RPM, clockwise.

questions.question: A belt connects a 10 cm diameter drive pulley to a 30 cm diameter driven pulley. If the drive pulley spins at 900 RPM, what is the speed of the driven pulley?
questions.explanation: Belt drive ratio = drive diameter / driven diameter = 10/30 = 1/3. Output speed = 900 x (1/3) = 300 RPM.

questions.question: Unlike meshing gears, two pulleys connected by a belt rotate in:
choices: The same direction only with a crossed belt
questions.explanation: Two pulleys connected by a standard (uncrossed) belt rotate in the same direction. A crossed belt would make them rotate in opposite directions.

questions.question: A worm gear makes one complete revolution. If the worm wheel has 40 teeth, how many degrees does the worm wheel rotate?
questions.explanation: One revolution of the worm advances the worm wheel by one tooth. With 40 teeth total, one tooth = 360/40 = 9 degrees.

questions.question: In a gear system, the idler gear's primary purpose is to:
choices: Change the direction of rotation without changing the gear ratio
questions.explanation: An idler gear changes the direction of rotation of the output gear without affecting the overall gear ratio (speed and torque relationship).

questions.question: A chain drive connects a 12-tooth front sprocket to a 36-tooth rear sprocket on a bicycle. For every one revolution of the pedals, how many times does the rear wheel turn?
questions.explanation: Gear ratio = rear teeth / front teeth = 36/12 = 3. The rear wheel turns at 1/3 the rate of the pedals, so one pedal revolution produces 1/3 revolution of the rear wheel.

questions.question: A gear system has an input gear spinning at 1200 RPM with a torque of 5 N-m. Assuming 100% efficiency, if the output gear spins at 300 RPM, what is the output torque?
questions.explanation: Power is conserved: input power = output power. RPM_in x torque_in = RPM_out x torque_out. 1200 x 5 = 300 x torque_out, so torque_out = 6000/300 = 20 N-m.

questions.question: In a hydraulic system, a force of 50 N is applied to a piston with an area of 10 sq cm. What is the pressure in the fluid?
questions.explanation: Pressure = force / area = 50 N / 10 sq cm = 5 N/sq cm.

questions.question: A hydraulic jack has an input piston area of 5 sq cm and an output piston area of 100 sq cm. If 200 N of force is applied to the input piston, what force is produced at the output?
questions.explanation: By Pascal's Law, pressure is equal throughout: P = 200/5 = 40 N/sq cm. Output force = P x output area = 40 x 100 = 4000 N.

questions.question: A solid block weighs 20 N in air and 12 N when fully submerged in water. What is the buoyant force acting on the block?
questions.explanation: Buoyant force = weight in air - apparent weight in water = 20 N - 12 N = 8 N.

questions.question: According to Pascal's Law, pressure applied to an enclosed fluid is:
choices: Absorbed by the walls of the container
Concentrated at the bottom of the container
Reduced by the volume of the fluid
questions.explanation: Pascal's Law states that pressure applied to a confined fluid is transmitted equally and undiminished in all directions throughout the fluid.

questions.question: A pipe narrows from a large diameter to a small diameter. According to the Continuity Equation, as the pipe narrows, the fluid velocity:
questions.explanation: The Continuity Equation (A1 x v1 = A2 x v2) states that as cross-sectional area decreases, velocity must increase to maintain constant flow rate.

questions.question: A 12 V battery is connected to a 4-ohm resistor. What current flows through the circuit?
questions.explanation: Ohm's Law: I = V/R = 12 V / 4 ohms = 3 A.

questions.question: Three resistors of 6 ohms each are connected in parallel. What is the total resistance?
questions.explanation: For equal resistors in parallel: total R = R/n = 6/3 = 2 ohms.

questions.question: A circuit has a 10-ohm and a 20-ohm resistor connected in series to a 30 V source. What is the voltage drop across the 20-ohm resistor?
questions.explanation: Total resistance = 10 + 20 = 30 ohms. Current = 30 V / 30 ohms = 1 A. Voltage across 20-ohm resistor = I x R = 1 x 20 = 20 V.

questions.question: A device draws 5 A of current from a 120 V outlet. What is the power consumed by the device?
questions.explanation: Power = voltage x current = 120 V x 5 A = 600 W.

questions.question: In a series circuit with three identical light bulbs, if one bulb burns out (open circuit), what happens to the other two?
choices: One goes out and one gets brighter
questions.explanation: In a series circuit, current follows a single path. If one bulb burns out and creates an open circuit, the path is broken and no current flows to any bulb.

questions.question: A metal spoon placed in a hot cup of soup quickly becomes warm. This is an example of heat transfer by:
questions.explanation: Conduction is heat transfer through direct contact between materials. The spoon conducts heat from the hot soup to your hand.

questions.question: Warm air rises from a floor heating vent and circulates through a room. This is an example of:
questions.explanation: Convection is heat transfer through the movement of fluids (including air). Warm air rises, cool air sinks, creating circulation currents.

questions.question: A steel bridge has expansion joints to allow for changes in temperature. When the temperature increases, the bridge:
choices: Stays the same length but gets thicker
Contracts in width but expands in length
questions.explanation: Thermal expansion causes materials to expand when heated. The bridge gets longer as temperature increases, which is why expansion joints are needed to prevent buckling.

questions.question: Two identical metal rods are heated from 20 degrees C to 80 degrees C. Rod A is made of aluminum (high thermal expansion) and Rod B is made of steel (low thermal expansion). Which rod expands more?
choices: Neither expands at this temperature range
questions.explanation: Aluminum has a higher coefficient of thermal expansion than steel. Given the same temperature change and identical dimensions, the aluminum rod expands more.

questions.question: A thermos bottle reduces heat transfer by minimizing all three modes. The vacuum between the walls primarily reduces heat transfer by:
questions.explanation: A vacuum eliminates conduction and convection because there are no molecules to transfer energy. Radiation can still pass through a vacuum, which is why reflective coatings are also used.

questions.question: When cutting sheet metal, which tool produces the smoothest, most precise straight cut?
questions.explanation: A bench shear (also called a foot shear or stomp shear) produces the smoothest, most precise straight cuts in sheet metal because it uses a fixed blade and a moving blade aligned along a straight edge.

questions.question: A torque wrench is primarily used to:
choices: Tighten fasteners to a specified tightness
questions.explanation: A torque wrench is specifically designed to apply a precise, measurable amount of torque to a fastener, ensuring it is tightened to the manufacturer's specification.

questions.question: Which type of fire extinguisher should be used on an electrical fire?
questions.explanation: Class C fire extinguishers (CO2 or dry chemical) are rated for electrical fires. Water (Class A) conducts electricity and is dangerous on electrical fires.

questions.question: A drill press is preferred over a handheld drill when you need:
choices: Precise perpendicular holes at consistent depths
The ability to drill into concrete walls
questions.explanation: A drill press holds the drill bit perpendicular to the workpiece and allows precise depth control, making it ideal for accurate, repeatable holes.

questions.question: When using a micrometer to measure the diameter of a shaft, the thimble is rotated until the spindle contacts the shaft. The measurement is read from:
choices: The sleeve scale and thimble scale combined
questions.explanation: A micrometer reading combines the main scale on the sleeve (which shows millimeters or 0.025-inch increments) with the thimble scale (which subdivides those increments for precise measurement).

questions.question: A cube is painted red on all six faces and then cut into 27 equal smaller cubes. How many of the smaller cubes have exactly two red faces?
questions.explanation: In a 3x3x3 cube, the smaller cubes with exactly two painted faces are the edge cubes (not corners). Each edge has 1 such cube, and a cube has 12 edges, giving 12 small cubes with exactly two red faces.

questions.question: A flat piece of cardboard shaped like a cross (one center square with four squares attached to each side) is folded into a box. Which face is opposite the center square?
choices: There is no opposite face; the center square becomes the bottom
The square directly across from the center
The box cannot be formed from this shape
None, the center square is open
questions.explanation: A cross-shaped net (one center square with four squares on each side) forms an open-top box. The center square becomes the bottom, and the four side squares fold up to form the walls. There is no face opposite the center (bottom) -- the top is open.

questions.question: A clock reads 3:15. What is the approximate angle between the hour hand and the minute hand?
questions.explanation: At 3:15, the minute hand points at the 3 (90 degrees from 12). The hour hand has moved past the 3 by 1/4 of the way toward the 4, which is 7.5 degrees. The angle between them is approximately 7.5 degrees.

questions.question: Looking at a standard six-sided die from one corner so that you can see three faces at once, you see the faces showing 1, 2, and 3. On a standard die, opposite faces add to 7. Which face is opposite the face showing 1?
questions.explanation: On a standard die, opposite faces sum to 7. The face opposite 1 is 6 (since 1 + 6 = 7).

questions.question: A rectangular piece of paper is folded in half, then in half again perpendicular to the first fold. A single hole is punched through all layers at the center. When the paper is fully unfolded, how many holes are visible?
questions.explanation: After two perpendicular folds, the paper has 4 layers. A single hole punched through all layers creates 4 holes when the paper is unfolded, arranged in a 2x2 pattern.

questions.question: A 50 kg crate sits on a flat surface with a coefficient of static friction of 0.4. What minimum horizontal force is needed to start moving the crate?
questions.explanation: Static friction = coefficient x normal force = 0.4 x (50 kg x 9.8 m/s2) = 0.4 x 490 N = 196 N, approximately 200 N.

questions.question: Two ropes pull an object in the same direction with forces of 30 N and 45 N. A third rope pulls in the opposite direction with 50 N. What is the net force on the object?
choices: 25 N in the direction of the two ropes
25 N in the direction of the single rope
125 N in the direction of the two ropes
15 N in the direction of the single rope
questions.explanation: Net force = (30 + 45) - 50 = 25 N in the direction of the two ropes.

questions.question: A ball is thrown straight up into the air. At the highest point of its trajectory, what is its acceleration?
choices: It depends on how hard the ball was thrown
questions.explanation: At the highest point, velocity is zero but acceleration due to gravity remains 9.8 m/s2 downward throughout the entire flight.

questions.question: A car traveling at 60 km/h has twice the kinetic energy of the same car traveling at what speed?
questions.explanation: Kinetic energy is proportional to v2. If KE2 = 2 x KE1, then v2 = v1 / sqrt(2) = 60 / 1.414 = 42.4 km/h.

questions.question: An elevator accelerates upward at 2 m/s2. A 70 kg person standing inside would feel:
questions.explanation: When an elevator accelerates upward, the normal force on the person increases beyond their weight, making them feel heavier. Apparent weight = m(g + a) = 70(9.8 + 2) = 826 N.

questions.question: A 10 kg box slides down a frictionless ramp inclined at 30 degrees. What is the acceleration of the box along the ramp?
questions.explanation: On a frictionless incline, acceleration = g x sin(30) = 9.8 x 0.5 = 4.9 m/s2.

questions.question: A truck and a compact car collide head-on. Which vehicle experiences the greater force during the collision?
choices: It depends on which vehicle was moving faster
questions.explanation: Newton's third law states that for every action there is an equal and opposite reaction. Both vehicles experience the same magnitude of force.

questions.question: A worker pushes a 40 kg cart with a constant force of 80 N across a smooth floor for 5 meters. How much work is done?
questions.explanation: Work = Force x distance = 80 N x 5 m = 400 J.

questions.question: A projectile is launched horizontally from a cliff. Ignoring air resistance, what happens to its horizontal velocity as it falls?
questions.explanation: With no air resistance, there is no horizontal force acting on the projectile. The horizontal velocity remains constant throughout the fall.

questions.question: A 5 kg block rests on a table. If a 20 N upward force is applied to it, what is the normal force from the table?
questions.explanation: Weight = mg = 5 x 9.8 = 49 N downward. The 20 N upward applied force reduces the force on the table. Normal force = 49 - 20 = 29 N.

questions.question: A merry-go-round spins at constant speed. A rider on the outer edge experiences:
choices: No acceleration because speed is constant
Acceleration in the direction of motion
questions.explanation: Uniform circular motion requires centripetal acceleration directed toward the center of the circle, even when speed is constant.

questions.question: A 2 kg ball moving at 3 m/s collides with a stationary 2 kg ball in a perfectly elastic collision. After the collision, the first ball:
questions.explanation: In a perfectly elastic collision between equal masses where one is initially stationary, the first object stops and transfers all its momentum and kinetic energy to the second.

questions.question: A crane lifts a 500 kg steel beam 12 meters in 20 seconds. What is the power output of the crane?
An object is in static equilibrium when:
choices: It is not moving and the net force is zero
It is moving at constant velocity
The net torque on it is zero but the net force is not
No forces are acting on it
questions.explanation: Static equilibrium means the object is at rest with both net force and net torque equal to zero.

questions.question: A spring with a spring constant of 200 N/m is compressed 0.1 m. How much potential energy is stored in the spring?
questions.explanation: Elastic potential energy = (1/2) x k x x2 = 0.5 x 200 x (0.1)2 = 0.5 x 200 x 0.01 = 1 J.

questions.question: A lever has its fulcrum placed 0.5 m from the load and 2.0 m from the effort. If the load weighs 400 N, what effort force is required to balance it?
questions.explanation: For a lever in equilibrium: Effort x effort arm = Load x load arm. Effort = (400 x 0.5) / 2.0 = 100 N.

questions.question: A worker uses a ramp 6 meters long to raise a barrel 1.5 meters onto a loading dock. What is the ideal mechanical advantage?
questions.explanation: IMA of a ramp = length / height = 6 / 1.5 = 4.

questions.question: A compound pulley system has 4 supporting ropes. Ignoring friction, how much force is needed to lift a 240 kg load?
questions.explanation: Force = Weight / number of supporting ropes = (240 x 9.8) / 4 = 2352 / 4 = 588 N.

questions.question: A wedge is essentially a moving version of which simple machine?
questions.explanation: A wedge is a portable inclined plane. It converts a force applied to its blunt end into forces perpendicular to its inclined surfaces.

questions.question: A wheelbarrow is an example of which class of lever?
questions.explanation: A wheelbarrow has the fulcrum at the wheel, the load in the middle, and the effort at the handles -- this is a second-class lever.

questions.question: A screw has 8 threads per centimeter. What is the pitch of the screw?
questions.explanation: Pitch = 1 / (threads per unit length) = 1 / 8 = 0.125 cm.

questions.question: A wheel with a radius of 30 cm is attached to an axle with a radius of 5 cm. What is the ideal mechanical advantage?
questions.explanation: IMA of a wheel and axle = radius of wheel / radius of axle = 30 / 5 = 6.

questions.question: Which simple machine would provide the greatest mechanical advantage for splitting a log?
questions.explanation: A wedge is the ideal simple machine for splitting. It converts a downward force into lateral forces that push the material apart.

questions.question: A block-and-tackle system with 6 supporting ropes is used to lift a 900 N load. The user must pull 6 meters of rope to raise the load by:
questions.explanation: With 6 supporting ropes, the load moves 1/6 of the rope pulled. Distance = 6 / 6 = 1.0 m.

questions.question: A machine has an actual mechanical advantage of 4 and an ideal mechanical advantage of 5. What is its efficiency?
questions.explanation: Efficiency = (AMA / IMA) x 100% = (4 / 5) x 100% = 80%.

questions.question: A driving gear with 15 teeth meshes with a driven gear with 45 teeth. If the driving gear rotates at 900 RPM, how fast does the driven gear turn?
questions.explanation: Gear ratio = driven teeth / driving teeth = 45 / 15 = 3. Output speed = 900 / 3 = 300 RPM.

questions.question: In a gear train with three meshing gears (A-B-C), gear A turns clockwise. Which direction does gear C turn?
choices: It depends on the number of teeth
questions.explanation: Each meshing gear reverses direction. Gear A (CW) drives gear B (CCW), which drives gear C (CW). With an odd number of gears, the last turns the same as the first.

questions.question: A belt connects two pulleys. Pulley A has a diameter of 10 cm and pulley B has a diameter of 40 cm. If pulley A spins at 1200 RPM, what is the speed of pulley B?
questions.explanation: For belt drives: speed ratio = diameter of A / diameter of B = 10 / 40 = 1/4. Speed of B = 1200 x (10/40) = 300 RPM.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of an idler gear in a gear train?
choices: To change the direction of rotation without affecting the gear ratio
questions.explanation: An idler gear reverses the direction of rotation of the output gear without changing the overall speed ratio between input and output.

questions.question: A worm gear system has a worm wheel with 40 teeth. If the worm has a single start, how many turns of the worm are needed for one full revolution of the wheel?
questions.explanation: For a single-start worm, each revolution of the worm advances the wheel by one tooth. So 40 worm turns are needed for one full revolution of the 40-tooth wheel.

questions.question: Two pulleys are connected by a crossed belt. If the driving pulley turns clockwise, the driven pulley turns:
choices: In the same direction only if they are the same size
Neither -- it depends on the belt tension
questions.explanation: A crossed belt reverses the direction of rotation. If the driving pulley turns clockwise, the driven pulley also turns clockwise (same direction as seen from the front of each pulley).

questions.question: A gear with 20 teeth drives a gear with 60 teeth, which is fixed to a shaft that also carries a gear with 15 teeth. That 15-tooth gear drives a final gear with 45 teeth. What is the overall gear ratio?
questions.explanation: First pair ratio: 60/20 = 3. Second pair ratio: 45/15 = 3. Overall ratio = 3 x 3 = 9:1.

questions.question: Which type of gear is used to transmit motion between shafts that are at 90 degrees to each other?
questions.explanation: Bevel gears are designed specifically to transmit motion between intersecting shafts, typically at 90 degrees.

questions.question: A V-belt is preferred over a flat belt in many applications because:
choices: It can transmit more power without slipping
questions.explanation: The wedge shape of a V-belt increases grip in the pulley groove, allowing it to transmit more power without slipping compared to a flat belt.

questions.question: A compound gear train reduces speed by a ratio of 8:1. If the input shaft delivers 2 N-m of torque, what is the theoretical output torque (ignoring losses)?
questions.explanation: In a gear reduction, output torque = input torque x gear ratio = 2 x 8 = 16 N-m. Speed decreases but torque increases proportionally.

questions.question: In a hydraulic press, the input piston has an area of 5 cm2 and the output piston has an area of 100 cm2. If a force of 50 N is applied to the input piston, what force is produced at the output?
questions.explanation: Pascal's principle: F_out = F_in x (A_out / A_in) = 50 x (100 / 5) = 50 x 20 = 1000 N.

questions.question: A pipe carrying water narrows from a diameter of 10 cm to 5 cm. According to the continuity equation, the velocity of water in the narrow section is:
choices: Half the velocity in the wide section
Twice the velocity in the wide section
Four times the velocity in the wide section
The same as the velocity in the wide section
questions.explanation: By continuity, A1 x v1 = A2 x v2. Area is proportional to diameter squared. The area ratio = (10/5)2 = 4, so the velocity increases by a factor of 4.

questions.question: A hydraulic cylinder has an input piston area of 2 cm2 and an output piston area of 40 cm2. If the input piston moves 20 cm, how far does the output piston move?
questions.explanation: Volume is conserved: A_in x d_in = A_out x d_out. d_out = (2 x 20) / 40 = 1.0 cm.

questions.question: What happens to the pressure at the bottom of a tank if the depth of the liquid is doubled?
questions.explanation: Hydrostatic pressure = density x g x depth. Pressure is directly proportional to depth, so doubling the depth doubles the pressure.

questions.question: An object weighs 10 N in air and 6 N when submerged in water. What is the buoyant force acting on the object?
questions.explanation: Buoyant force = weight in air - apparent weight in water = 10 - 6 = 4 N.

questions.question: Three resistors of 10 ohms, 20 ohms, and 30 ohms are connected in series across a 120 V battery. What is the total current in the circuit?
questions.explanation: Total resistance in series = 10 + 20 + 30 = 60 ohms. Current = V / R = 120 / 60 = 2 A.

questions.question: Two identical resistors are connected in parallel. Compared to a single resistor, the total resistance of the parallel combination is:
questions.explanation: Two identical resistors R in parallel: total = R/2. The parallel combination has half the resistance of a single resistor.

questions.question: A 100 W light bulb runs for 5 hours. How much electrical energy does it consume?
questions.explanation: Energy = Power x time = 100 W x 5 h = 500 Wh = 0.5 kWh.

questions.question: In a circuit, a fuse is designed to:
choices: Increase voltage when demand is high
Store electrical energy for later use
Break the circuit if current exceeds a safe level
questions.explanation: A fuse is a safety device that melts and breaks the circuit when current exceeds a rated level, protecting wiring and equipment from damage.

questions.question: A wire carries a current of 3 A through a resistance of 8 ohms. What is the voltage drop across the wire?
questions.explanation: Ohm's law: V = I x R = 3 x 8 = 24 V.

questions.question: Heat transfer through direct contact between two objects is called:
questions.explanation: Conduction is the transfer of heat through direct molecular contact between two objects or within a solid material.

questions.question: A metal rod is heated at one end. Which metal would conduct the heat to the other end most quickly?
questions.explanation: Copper has the highest thermal conductivity among the listed metals (approximately 400 W/m-K), making it the fastest conductor of heat.

questions.question: When a gas is compressed in a sealed container, its temperature:
choices: Depends on the color of the container
questions.explanation: Compressing a gas does work on it, increasing its internal energy and therefore its temperature. This follows from the first law of thermodynamics.

questions.question: A bimetallic strip bends when heated because:
choices: Both metals expand at the same rate
The two metals expand at different rates
One metal contracts while the other expands
The strip absorbs more heat on one side
questions.explanation: A bimetallic strip is made of two metals with different coefficients of thermal expansion. When heated, one metal expands more than the other, causing the strip to bend.

questions.question: Which process transfers heat primarily through the movement of fluid currents?
questions.explanation: Convection transfers heat through bulk movement of fluid (liquid or gas). Warmer fluid rises while cooler fluid sinks, creating convection currents.

questions.question: A torque wrench is used to:
choices: Cut bolts to the correct length
Tighten fasteners to a specific torque value
questions.explanation: A torque wrench is a precision tool that allows the user to apply a specific, measurable amount of torque to a fastener, preventing over-tightening or under-tightening.

questions.question: Which material is most resistant to corrosion in a saltwater environment?
questions.explanation: Stainless steel contains chromium, which forms a passive oxide layer that resists corrosion, making it the best choice for saltwater environments among the options listed.

questions.question: When drilling into hardened steel, the drill speed should be:
questions.explanation: Hardened steel requires low drill speed to prevent overheating and heavy pressure to maintain cutting action. High speed would dull the drill bit quickly.

questions.question: A micrometer measures to an accuracy of:
questions.explanation: A standard micrometer can measure to an accuracy of 0.01 mm (10 micrometers), making it one of the most precise handheld measuring instruments.

questions.question: The process of heating metal to a specific temperature and then cooling it slowly to reduce brittleness is called:
questions.explanation: Annealing is the process of heating metal to a specific temperature and then cooling it slowly to relieve internal stresses, reduce hardness, and increase ductility.

questions.question: A flat pattern is folded into a cube. If the bottom face has a circle, which face is directly opposite?
choices: The face that was adjacent to the left of the circle in the flat pattern
The face that was two squares away in a straight line from the circle
The face that was adjacent to the right of the circle in the flat pattern
It cannot be determined without seeing the pattern
questions.explanation: In a standard cross-shaped cube net, the face directly opposite any given face is the one that is two squares away in a straight line.

questions.question: A cylindrical pipe is cut at a 45-degree angle. The shape of the cross-section is:
questions.explanation: When a cylinder is cut at any angle other than perpendicular to its axis, the cross-section is an ellipse. A perpendicular cut gives a circle.

questions.question: A gear viewed from the front appears to rotate clockwise. When viewed from the back (opposite side), the same gear appears to rotate:
choices: The same direction regardless of viewing angle
It depends on the number of teeth
questions.explanation: A gear rotating clockwise when viewed from the front appears counterclockwise when viewed from the back. The rotation itself does not change, but the viewing perspective is mirrored.

questions.question: A solid rectangular block is 4 cm long, 3 cm wide, and 2 cm tall. How many faces does the block have?
questions.explanation: A rectangular block (rectangular prism) always has 6 faces: top, bottom, front, back, left, and right.

questions.question: If you look at the letter R in a mirror, which part of the letter appears reversed?
choices: The vertical stroke moves to the right side
The bump and leg of the R face left instead of right
The letter does not change in a mirror
questions.explanation: A mirror reverses left and right. The vertical stroke of R stays on the left, but the bump and diagonal leg, which normally face right, appear to face left.

questions.question: A 20-kg crate sits on a flat surface. If a horizontal force of 50 N is applied and the crate does not move, what can be concluded?
choices: The applied force exceeds the static friction force
The static friction force is at least 50 N
The kinetic friction force equals 50 N
The crate has no friction acting on it
questions.explanation: Since the crate does not move, the static friction force must be at least equal to the 50 N applied force, balancing it completely.

questions.question: A ball is thrown straight upward. At the very top of its flight, what is its acceleration?
choices: It depends on the mass of the ball
questions.explanation: At the top of its flight the ball's velocity is momentarily zero, but gravity still acts on it, producing a downward acceleration of 9.8 m/s2.

questions.question: Two ropes are attached to a heavy box. Rope A pulls north with 40 N and Rope B pulls east with 30 N. What is the approximate magnitude of the resultant force?
questions.explanation: The resultant is found using the Pythagorean theorem: sqrt(40^2 + 30^2) = sqrt(1600 + 900) = sqrt(2500) = 50 N.

questions.question: A truck traveling at 60 km/h has twice the mass of a car traveling at 60 km/h. How does the truck's momentum compare to the car's?
choices: The truck's momentum is the same as the car's
The truck's momentum is twice the car's
The truck's momentum is four times the car's
The truck's momentum is half the car's
questions.explanation: Momentum equals mass times velocity. Since both travel at the same speed and the truck has twice the mass, the truck has twice the momentum.

questions.question: A worker pushes a filing cabinet across a floor at constant velocity. What can be said about the net force on the cabinet?
choices: The net force is in the direction of motion
The net force is opposite the direction of motion
The net force equals the worker's applied force
questions.explanation: Constant velocity means zero acceleration, which by Newton's second law means the net force is zero. The applied force exactly balances friction.

questions.question: A 5-kg object accelerates at 3 m/s2. What is the net force acting on it?
questions.explanation: Using F = ma: F = 5 kg x 3 m/s2 = 15 N.

questions.question: A pendulum swings back and forth. At which point in its swing is the speed greatest?
choices: At the highest point on either side
At the lowest point of the swing
Midway between the lowest and highest points
Speed is constant throughout the swing
questions.explanation: A pendulum converts potential energy to kinetic energy. Kinetic energy (and therefore speed) is greatest at the lowest point of the swing.

questions.question: A car rounds a curve at constant speed. Which statement is true?
choices: No force acts on the car
A net force acts toward the center of the curve
A net force acts away from the center of the curve
questions.explanation: Circular motion requires a centripetal force directed toward the center of the curve. The car's speed is constant but its velocity (which includes direction) is changing.

questions.question: An astronaut in the International Space Station appears weightless. This is because:
choices: There is no gravity in space
The station and astronaut are in free fall together
The station shields the astronaut from gravity
Air pressure inside the station cancels gravity
questions.explanation: Gravity still acts in orbit. The astronaut and station are both in free fall (continuous falling around the Earth), so the astronaut appears weightless.

questions.question: A spring with a spring constant of 200 N/m is compressed 0.1 m. What force does the spring exert?
questions.explanation: Hooke's law: F = kx = 200 N/m x 0.1 m = 20 N.

questions.question: Object A has a mass of 2 kg and Object B has a mass of 8 kg. They are both dropped from the same height in a vacuum. Which hits the ground first?
choices: Object A, because it is lighter
Object B, because it is heavier
They hit at the same time
questions.explanation: In a vacuum with no air resistance, all objects fall at the same rate regardless of mass, due to the equivalence of inertial and gravitational mass.

questions.question: A hockey puck slides across frictionless ice at 5 m/s. If no forces act on it, what happens?
choices: It gradually slows down and stops
It accelerates in the forward direction
It continues at 5 m/s in the same direction
questions.explanation: Newton's first law: an object in motion with no net force acting on it continues at constant velocity. On frictionless ice, no force decelerates the puck.

questions.question: A crane lifts a 500-kg steel beam vertically at constant velocity. What is the tension in the cable? (Use g = 10 m/s2)
questions.explanation: At constant velocity the net force is zero, so the cable tension equals the beam's weight: T = mg = 500 x 10 = 5000 N.

questions.question: A bicycle rider goes over a hill. At the very top of the hill, the rider feels lighter than normal. Why?
choices: Gravity is weaker at higher elevations
Part of the gravitational force provides centripetal acceleration
Air resistance reduces the rider's weight
The rider's inertia increases at the top
questions.explanation: At the top of the hill, the rider follows a curved path. Part of gravity provides the centripetal force needed for circular motion, reducing the normal force and making the rider feel lighter.

questions.question: Two ice skaters stand facing each other on frictionless ice. Skater A pushes Skater B. Which statement is correct?
choices: Both skaters move apart in opposite directions
Skater A moves but Skater B stays still
questions.explanation: Newton's third law: the push on Skater B creates an equal and opposite force on Skater A. On frictionless ice, both skaters accelerate away from each other.

questions.question: A lever has its fulcrum placed 0.5 m from the load and 2 m from the effort. What is the mechanical advantage?
questions.explanation: Mechanical advantage = effort arm / load arm = 2 m / 0.5 m = 4.

questions.question: A worker uses a ramp to load barrels onto a truck. If the ramp is 6 m long and the truck bed is 1.5 m high, what is the ideal mechanical advantage?
questions.explanation: Ideal MA of a ramp = length / height = 6 m / 1.5 m = 4.

questions.question: Which class of lever has the fulcrum between the effort and the load?
questions.explanation: A first-class lever has the fulcrum between the effort and the load, like a seesaw.

questions.question: A wheel and axle system has a wheel radius of 30 cm and an axle radius of 5 cm. What is the mechanical advantage?
questions.explanation: MA = wheel radius / axle radius = 30 cm / 5 cm = 6.

questions.question: A fixed pulley is used to lift a 100-N weight. How much force must be applied (ignoring friction)?
questions.explanation: A single fixed pulley changes the direction of the force but does not provide a mechanical advantage. The effort equals the load: 100 N.

questions.question: A screw has 8 threads per centimeter. Compared to a screw with 4 threads per centimeter, the finer-threaded screw:
choices: Requires less force per turn but advances less per turn
Requires more force per turn and advances more per turn
Requires the same force regardless of thread count
questions.explanation: A finer thread pitch means more threads per unit length. Each turn advances the screw less distance, but requires less force -- a higher mechanical advantage.

questions.question: A compound machine is best described as:
choices: A machine with no moving parts
Two or more simple machines working together
A machine with a mechanical advantage of exactly 1
questions.explanation: A compound machine is a combination of two or more simple machines working together, such as a pair of scissors (two first-class levers with wedge-shaped blades).

questions.question: A block-and-tackle system uses 4 supporting rope segments. Approximately how much force is needed to lift a 400-N load (ignoring friction)?
questions.explanation: In a block-and-tackle with 4 supporting segments, the mechanical advantage is 4. Force = 400 N / 4 = 100 N.

questions.question: A wheelbarrow is an example of which class of lever?
questions.explanation: A wheelbarrow is a second-class lever: the load is between the fulcrum (wheel) and the effort (handles).

questions.question: When using any simple machine, which of the following is always true (assuming no friction)?
choices: Power output is greater than power input
questions.explanation: Conservation of energy dictates that work output equals work input in an ideal (frictionless) machine. Force and distance can differ, but their products (work) are equal.

questions.question: A driving gear has 15 teeth and meshes with a driven gear that has 45 teeth. If the driving gear turns at 600 RPM, how fast does the driven gear turn?
questions.explanation: Gear ratio = driven/driving = 45/15 = 3. Output RPM = 600/3 = 200 RPM. A larger driven gear turns slower.

questions.question: In a standard spur gear train, when Gear A meshes directly with Gear B, how do they rotate relative to each other?
choices: They rotate in the same direction
The direction depends on the number of teeth
questions.explanation: Two meshing spur gears always rotate in opposite directions. The teeth push against each other, reversing the rotation.

questions.question: A belt connects Pulley X (diameter 10 cm) to Pulley Y (diameter 40 cm). If Pulley X rotates at 800 RPM, what is the speed of Pulley Y?
questions.explanation: Belt drives: speed is inversely proportional to diameter. RPM_Y = RPM_X x (D_X / D_Y) = 800 x (10/40) = 200 RPM.

questions.question: In a gear train with three gears in a line (A meshes with B, B meshes with C), how does the rotation of Gear C compare to Gear A?
questions.explanation: Each meshing pair reverses direction. A to B reverses once, B to C reverses again. Two reversals return C to the same direction as A. The middle gear (B) acts as an idler.

questions.question: A worm gear arrangement consists of a worm with 1 start and a worm wheel with 40 teeth. What is the gear ratio?
questions.explanation: For a single-start worm, the gear ratio equals the number of teeth on the worm wheel. One turn of the worm advances the wheel by one tooth, so the ratio is 40:1.

questions.question: If a gear system reduces speed by a factor of 5, what happens to the torque (ignoring friction)?
choices: Torque is reduced by a factor of 5
Torque increases by a factor of 5
Torque increases by a factor of 25
questions.explanation: In an ideal gear system, power is conserved (Power = Torque x Speed). If speed decreases by a factor of 5, torque must increase by a factor of 5.

questions.question: A crossed belt between two pulleys causes them to:
questions.explanation: A crossed (twisted) belt causes the two pulleys to rotate in opposite directions, unlike an uncrossed belt where both rotate the same way.

questions.question: An idler gear placed between a driving gear and a driven gear primarily serves to:
choices: Change the direction of rotation without affecting the gear ratio
questions.explanation: An idler gear changes the direction of rotation of the driven gear without changing the overall gear ratio between the first and last gear.

questions.question: Bevel gears are used to:
choices: Transmit rotation between parallel shafts only
questions.explanation: Bevel gears have conically shaped tooth surfaces and are designed to transmit rotation between shafts that intersect, typically at 90 degrees.

questions.question: A chain drive (like a bicycle chain) compared to a belt drive:
choices: Is more likely to slip under load
Provides positive engagement and does not slip
Always rotates pulleys in opposite directions
questions.explanation: A chain drive uses positive engagement between the chain links and sprocket teeth, so it cannot slip under load, unlike a friction-dependent belt drive.

questions.question: In a hydraulic jack, the small piston has an area of 2 cm2 and the large piston has an area of 50 cm2. If you apply 20 N to the small piston, what force is produced at the large piston?
Water flows through a pipe that narrows from a wide section to a narrow section. According to the continuity equation, what happens to the flow velocity?
choices: Velocity decreases in the narrow section
Velocity increases in the narrow section
Velocity drops to zero in the narrow section
questions.explanation: The continuity equation (A1 x v1 = A2 x v2) states that for an incompressible fluid, velocity must increase when the cross-sectional area decreases.

questions.question: An object is fully submerged in water. The buoyant force on the object equals:
choices: The weight of the water displaced by the object
The volume of the object times the density of the object
The atmospheric pressure times the surface area
questions.explanation: Archimedes' principle: the buoyant force on a submerged object equals the weight of the fluid displaced by the object.

questions.question: In a hydraulic system, why is the fluid considered virtually incompressible?
choices: So that pressure is transmitted equally in all directions
So that the fluid can be heated easily
So that air bubbles form inside the system
So that the system weighs less
questions.explanation: Hydraulic systems rely on incompressible fluid so that pressure applied at one point is transmitted equally and instantly in all directions (Pascal's law).

questions.question: A tank is filled with water. At which point is the water pressure greatest?
choices: At the surface of the water
At the bottom of the tank
Pressure is the same at all depths
questions.explanation: Water pressure increases with depth: P = rho x g x h. The greatest depth (at the bottom of the tank) produces the greatest pressure.

questions.question: Three resistors of 6 ohms each are connected in parallel. What is the total resistance?
questions.explanation: For identical resistors in parallel: total R = R/n = 6/3 = 2 ohms.

questions.question: A 120-V circuit has a 10-A fuse. What is the maximum power that can be drawn before the fuse blows?
questions.explanation: Power = Voltage x Current = 120 V x 10 A = 1200 W.

questions.question: In a series circuit with three bulbs, one bulb burns out. What happens?
choices: The other two bulbs get brighter
The other two bulbs remain unchanged
questions.explanation: In a series circuit, current flows through a single path. If one bulb burns out, the circuit is broken and all bulbs go out.

questions.question: A wire's resistance increases when:
choices: Its length decreases and its cross-section increases
Its length increases and its cross-section decreases
Its length decreases and its cross-section decreases
questions.explanation: Resistance is proportional to length and inversely proportional to cross-sectional area (R = rho x L / A). Increasing length and decreasing area both increase resistance.

questions.question: An electric motor converts:
questions.explanation: An electric motor converts electrical energy into mechanical energy (rotational motion). A generator does the reverse.

questions.question: A metal rod is heated at one end. Heat travels to the other end primarily by:
questions.explanation: In a solid rod, heat transfers primarily by conduction -- the transfer of thermal energy through direct molecular collisions along the material.

questions.question: When a gas in a sealed rigid container is heated, what happens?
choices: The mass of the gas increases
questions.explanation: In a rigid (constant volume) sealed container, heating the gas increases the kinetic energy of the molecules. With volume fixed, the pressure increases (Gay-Lussac's law).

questions.question: A bimetallic strip bends when heated because:
choices: Both metals expand at the same rate
The two metals expand at different rates
One metal contracts while the other stays the same
questions.explanation: A bimetallic strip is made of two metals with different coefficients of thermal expansion. When heated, one metal expands more than the other, causing the strip to bend.

questions.question: On a hot day, concrete sidewalks have expansion joints. These joints are needed because:
choices: Concrete expands when heated and could crack without room to grow
The joints make the sidewalk look uniform
questions.explanation: Concrete expands when heated. Expansion joints provide space for this growth, preventing cracking and buckling.

questions.question: Which method of heat transfer does NOT require a medium (material) to travel through?
questions.explanation: Radiation (electromagnetic waves) can travel through a vacuum. Conduction requires direct contact between particles, and convection requires a fluid medium.

questions.question: A torque wrench is primarily used to:
choices: Measure the diameter of a bolt
Tighten fasteners to a specific torque value
questions.explanation: A torque wrench measures and limits the torque applied to a fastener, ensuring bolts are tightened to the manufacturer's specification.

questions.question: Which fastener is designed to resist vibration loosening by using a prevailing torque feature?
questions.explanation: A lock nut with a nylon insert creates a prevailing torque that resists loosening from vibration, unlike a standard hex nut.

questions.question: When drilling through metal, cutting fluid is used primarily to:
choices: Increase the hardness of the drill bit
Reduce friction and cool the drill bit
Prevent the metal from being magnetic
questions.explanation: Cutting fluid (coolant) reduces friction between the drill bit and workpiece, removes heat, and helps evacuate chips from the hole.

questions.question: An alloy is best described as:
choices: A pure metal that has been polished
A mixture of two or more metals or a metal and another element
A metal that cannot conduct electricity
A naturally occurring metal found in the earth
questions.explanation: An alloy is a mixture of two or more metals, or a metal with another element (e.g., steel is iron alloyed with carbon), designed to improve properties like strength or corrosion resistance.

questions.question: A cotter pin (split pin) inserted through a castle nut and bolt is used to:
choices: Prevent the nut from turning off the bolt
Allow the bolt to stretch under load
questions.explanation: A cotter pin is inserted through a hole in the bolt and the slots of a castle nut. It physically prevents the nut from rotating off, providing a positive locking mechanism.

questions.question: A cube is painted red on all six faces and then cut into 27 equal smaller cubes. How many of the smaller cubes have exactly two painted faces?
questions.explanation: Edge cubes have exactly two painted faces. A 3x3x3 cube has 12 edge positions (each edge has 1 middle piece, and there are 12 edges), so 12 smaller cubes have exactly two painted faces.

questions.question: A flat sheet of metal is folded into an open-top box by cutting equal squares from each corner and folding up the sides. If you cut larger squares from the corners, the resulting box will be:
choices: The same height with a larger base
questions.explanation: Cutting larger squares from the corners means the folded-up sides are taller, but the remaining base is smaller. The box becomes taller with a smaller base area.

questions.question: A cylindrical pipe is cut at a 45-degree angle. When the cut surface is viewed directly from above (looking straight down), it appears as:
questions.explanation: When viewed from directly above (top-down), the outline of a cylinder is always a circle, regardless of how the pipe was cut. The elliptical shape is only visible when viewing the cut surface at an angle.

questions.question: A gear is viewed from the front and appears to rotate clockwise. If you walk around and view the same gear from the back, it appears to rotate:
choices: It appears stationary from the back
The direction depends on the gear's size
questions.explanation: Viewing a rotating object from the opposite side reverses the apparent direction of rotation. What appears clockwise from the front appears counterclockwise from the back.

questions.question: A mirror is placed to the right of an object. In the mirror, the object's left side appears:
choices: On the left side of the reflection
On the right side of the reflection
At the top of the reflection
questions.explanation: A mirror reverses the image along the axis perpendicular to its surface. With the mirror to the right, the object's left side appears on the right side of the reflection.
